text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project. A good method is to use SMART goals. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." To track progress, break the project into milestones, such as completing the design phase or finishing user testing. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment—everyone involved should agree on the goals and expectations before work begins. This helps avoid misunderstandings later.
